topic.what: Plug-and-play inference-time defense against backdoor attacks in LLMs using Tail-risk Intrinsic Geometric Smoothing (TIGS)
topic.how: Detects trigger-induced attention collapse patterns via content-domain screening and applies intrinsic geometric smoothing to disrupt trigger-dominant routing without parameter updates
topic.why_matters: Provides deployment-ready backdoor mitigation that avoids expensive offline purification, clean data requirements, and multi-pass latency while preserving clean utility

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of backdoor activation successful with trigger inputs
characteristics.evidence: experimental evaluation across multiple attack families
characteristics.context: across dense reasoning-oriented and sparse MoE models

characteristics.what_it_measures: Additional latency added to prefill stage computation
Ensuring valid attention matrix after intervention

novelty: Detector-executor design separating content-domain detection from full-row execution
Tail-risk screening using extreme statistics rather than layer-wide averages
Prior-free geometric smoothing without external clean reference data
Minimum-KL full-row reconstruction under prescribed mass constraints
open_problems: Residual value and MLP-side pathways may require additional mitigation
Sufficiently capable adversaries could distribute trigger activation across heads
Positioned as deployment-time mitigation raising adversary cost not complete coverage
Does not claim coverage of all possible internal backdoor pathways
